coaching.coaching: Research prior art / OSS for actual retry patterns in this package before building it custom.
coaching.subject: actual retry patterns in this package

coaching.coaching: Pin down concrete retry count and backoff specification with a concrete worked example.
coaching.subject: concrete retry count and backoff specification

coaching.coaching: State the concrete attestation evidence the [operator-attested] actual xcheck call-site function names will require (a change id / vote outcome / timestamp), recorded on the ticket.
coaching.subject: actual xcheck call-site function names

coaching.coaching: State the concrete attestation evidence the [operator-attested] workflow_ops plan_review_coach context assembly path will require (a change id / vote outcome / timestamp), recorded on the ticket.
coaching.subject: workflow_ops plan_review_coach context assembly path

coaching.coaching: Pin down plumbing path from assembly to coverage dict with a concrete worked example.
coaching.subject: plumbing path from assembly to coverage dict

coaching.coaching: Realign parent epic carve-out for irrelevant hierarchy gaps to the parent's plan — the parent wins on conflict; if the parent is genuinely wrong, update the PARENT first (which forces its re-review), never silently diverge the leaf.
coaching.subject: parent epic carve-out for irrelevant hierarchy gaps

coaching.coaching: Generalize coverage-flag mirror invariant across all verdict sites across the rest of the work.
coaching.subject: coverage-flag mirror invariant across all verdict sites

coaching.coaching: Plan how runnable test commands and file paths will be verified in-session — restate any deferred or unobservable success target as an observable proxy.
coaching.subject: runnable test commands and file paths

coaching.coaching: Pin down hierarchy_incomplete_detail field population in tests with a concrete worked example.
coaching.subject: hierarchy_incomplete_detail field population in tests

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the non-existent 'verify.py's chunk retry loop' retry exemplar in Remediation §2 with the actual bounded-retry pattern location (runner.py:510-582 / structured.py:294) or describe the retry semantics directly (e.g. 'up to N attempts, same as the structured-output single-reask budget').
evidence: Plan text (Remediation §2): 'matching the retry pattern already used elsewhere in this package, e.g. verify.py's chunk retry loop'
review_kernel/verify.py:307-330: chunks are processed in a for-loop; a StructuredOutputError is caught and the chunk degrades — no retry of the chunk occurs
runner.py:510-582: the single bounded structured-output retry exists here (one reask to the same model with the validation error fed back)
structured.py:294: '# Default bounded retry budget for the structured-output path (layer 4): ONE retry'
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'matching the retry pattern already used elsewhere in this package, e.g. verify.py's chunk retry loop' as the model for the bounded-retry requirement, but verify.py has no chunk retry loop. Its chunk processing is a plain for-loop that catches StructuredOutputError and moves on (no retry). The actual bounded-retry pattern in this package lives at the structured-output/runner layer (runner.py:510-582, structured.py:294). Pointing at a non-existent retry pattern leaves the implementation requirement ambiguous.
findings.location: Remediation step 2 — 'matching the retry pattern already used elsewhere in this package, e.g. verify.py's chunk retry loop'
scenarios: An implementer who looks for a 'chunk retry loop' in verify.py to copy as the retry pattern will not find one and may either implement retry incorrectly (e.g. unbounded) or skip the retry requirement entirely.
Review of the implementation cannot validate 'matching the existing pattern' when the cited exemplar does not exist.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the named call sites in Remediation Step 4 and AC4 from `xcheck.apply_contradictions`/`xcheck.apply_comment_trail` to the actual function names `xcheck.apply_contradiction_drops` (xcheck.py:126) and `xcheck.apply_comment_trail_drops` (xcheck.py:258).
evidence: Plan says: 'via any of its three call sites (drift_floor.apply_to_verdict, xcheck.apply_contradictions, xcheck.apply_comment_trail)'
Grep: `def apply_contradiction_drops` at xcheck.py:126; no `apply_contradictions` anywhere in the package.
Grep: `def apply_comment_trail_drops` at xcheck.py:258; no `apply_comment_trail` anywhere in the package.
AC4 repeats the same incorrect names: 'xcheck.apply_contradictions, xcheck.apply_comment_trail'
findings.finding: The plan's Remediation Step 4 and AC4 name `xcheck.apply_contradictions` and `xcheck.apply_comment_trail` as the two xcheck call sites that invoke `_recompute_verdict_after_drop`, but neither function exists under those names. The actual functions are `xcheck.apply_contradiction_drops` (xcheck.py:126) and `xcheck.apply_comment_trail_drops` (xcheck.py:258).
findings.location: Remediation Step 4; AC line 'drift_floor.apply_to_verdict, xcheck.apply_contradictions, xcheck.apply_comment_trail'
scenarios: A developer tasked with this ticket searches for `xcheck.apply_contradictions` to add the hierarchy_incomplete branch and finds nothing, causing confusion or an incorrect edit to the wrong function.
findings.suggested_fix: In Remediation Step 4 and AC4, replace `xcheck.apply_contradictions` → `xcheck.apply_contradiction_drops` and `xcheck.apply_comment_trail` → `xcheck.apply_comment_trail_drops`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `workflow_ops.plan_review_coach` (and any intermediate step-input schemas) as a required edit target: the `hierarchy_incomplete` flag set in `assemble_context` must be propagated through the workflow step inputs so it is present in `coverage` when `finalize_verdict` is called from `plan_review_coach`.
evidence: workflow_ops.py:540-545: `pctx = PlanContext(ticket_id=..., ticket_type=..., title='', description='')` — no children, no store read.
workflow_ops.py:546-556: coverage dict built only from `det_coverage`, `routing`, `llm_ran`, and optional violations — no `hierarchy_incomplete` key.
orchestrator._assemble_context_uncached (orchestrator.py:163-211) is the only place that reads children and where the new flag would be set.
The plan does not name workflow_ops.py as an edit target, nor does it name the step-input schema change needed to thread the flag from assemble_context to finalize_verdict.
findings.finding: `plan_review_coach` in `workflow_ops.py` constructs a bare minimal `PlanContext` (no children, no store read — confirmed at workflow_ops.py:540–545) and assembles its own `coverage` dict from step inputs (workflow_ops.py:546–556). The `hierarchy_incomplete` flag will be set inside `orchestrator._assemble_context_uncached`, which runs in earlier steps (e.g. `plan_review_precheck`). To reach `finalize_verdict` inside `plan_review_coach`, the flag must be threaded through as a workflow step input. The plan names `finalize_verdict` as the sole edit target but does not enumerate `plan_review_coach`'s step-input schema or the upstream steps that must propagate `hierarchy_incomplete` as an unenumerated consumer.
findings.location: Remediation Step 3 (finalize_verdict branch); AC3; workflow_ops.py:503–564
scenarios: The hierarchy_incomplete flag is set in assemble_context but never reaches finalize_verdict inside plan_review_coach because coverage is built from step inputs that don't carry it — the new INDETERMINATE guard is silently bypassed on the main LLM-review path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the `verify.py` retry-pattern reference with the actual file/function in the package that implements bounded retries (or state the retry budget explicitly, e.g. 'up to N=3 attempts with exponential back-off') so the implementer has a concrete model to follow.
evidence: list_directory shows no verify.py under src/rebar/llm/plan_review/
Plan quotes: 'a small bounded retry (matching the retry pattern already used elsewhere in this package, e.g. verify.py's chunk retry loop)'
findings.finding: The plan references 'verify.py's chunk retry loop' as the retry pattern to match, but `verify.py` does not exist in the package file listing — there is no such module to imitate, leaving the bounded-retry implementation without a concrete model.
findings.location: Remediation §2: 'matching the retry pattern already used elsewhere in this package, e.g. verify.py's chunk retry loop'
scenarios: Implementer searches for verify.py to understand the retry pattern; file is absent; must guess at the retry budget and style, risking inconsistency with the rest of the package.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'e.g. verify.py's chunk retry loop' with the actual retry locus in the package (search for retry/backoff in orchestrator.py or passes.py), or state the retry budget directly (e.g. 'up to 3 attempts with a 1s/2s back-off, consistent with the pattern in <actual_file>:<line>').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact plumbing path for `coverage['hierarchy_incomplete']`: either (a) add an `assembly_coverage: dict` field to `PlanContext` that the orchestrator merges into the verdict's `coverage` dict before calling `finalize_verdict`, or (b) have `_assemble_context_uncached` / `assemble_context` accept a mutable `coverage` dict to write into — the plan must name one path so the flag reliably reaches `finalize_verdict`.
evidence: _assemble_context_uncached (orchestrator.py:163-211) returns a PlanContext dataclass; it does not accept or return a coverage dict.
finalize_verdict (orchestrator.py:503) receives coverage as an independent caller-supplied dict parameter.
PlanContext dataclass fields (det_floor.py:103-138) include no coverage slot.
Plan says 'PlanContext/the verdict's coverage dict' with a slash, ambiguously conflating two separate objects without specifying how the flag travels between them.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify the plumbing path by which `coverage['hierarchy_incomplete']` flows from the assembly step (`_assemble_context_uncached`, which returns a `PlanContext` with no `coverage` dict) into the `coverage` dict that is passed to `finalize_verdict`. These are two separate objects with different lifecycles; without a stated connection, the mechanism is incomplete.
findings.location: Remediation §1 / AC line 1: 'PlanContext/the verdict's coverage dict records whether child enumeration… via a new coverage["hierarchy_incomplete"] flag'
scenarios: Implementer sets coverage['hierarchy_incomplete'] inside _assemble_context_uncached but has nowhere to put it — PlanContext has no coverage field. They must either add a field to PlanContext and then extract it into the coverage dict at the call site in the orchestrator, or thread a mutable coverage dict into the assembly function. Neither path is specified.
If different implementers make different choices (PlanContext field vs. mutable coverage dict threading) the flag may be lost or double-set.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the broken reference to 'verify.py's chunk retry loop' with an accurate pointer to an existing pattern (e.g. `src/rebar/llm/runner.py`'s `OUTPUT_RETRIES` loop structure, or specify the retry count and exception-handling contract explicitly inline), since no `verify.py` exists in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/`.
evidence: Plan text: 'recoverable child-list/child-fetch errors ... get a small bounded retry (matching the retry pattern already used elsewhere in this package, e.g. verify.py's chunk retry loop)'
src/rebar/llm/plan_review/ directory listing: no verify.py present
src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py:302 contains 'bounded retry' but it retries LLM structured output parsing, not ticket-store reads
ABSENCE: no ticket-store read retry pattern exists anywhere in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/
findings.finding: The plan references 'verify.py's chunk retry loop' as the bounded-retry pattern to follow, but no verify.py exists in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/. The only verify.py in the codebase is src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py, and its retry loop retries LLM structured-output parsing failures — a structurally different operation from retrying a ticket-store read. An implementer following this reference has no concrete model for how to build a bounded retry around a tracker-store list/fetch call.
findings.location: Remediation step 2: 'matching the retry pattern already used elsewhere in this package, e.g. verify.py's chunk retry loop'
scenarios: Implementer searches for verify.py in the plan_review package, finds nothing, and either copies the wrong retry pattern (LLM output retry) or invents an ad-hoc one inconsistent with the codebase.
The retry mechanism for tracker reads is left out entirely because the referenced exemplar is unfindable.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace: 'matching the retry pattern already used elsewhere in this package, e.g. verify.py's chunk retry loop' with: 'using a simple for-loop retry (e.g. `for attempt in range(MAX_HIERARCHY_RETRIES):` where `MAX_HIERARCHY_RETRIES = 3`) with exponential backoff on the caught exception, consistent with the structured-output retry budget in `src/rebar/llm/runner.py` (`OUTPUT_RETRIES`).' Also define `MAX_HIERARCHY_RETRIES` as a named constant in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Disambiguate where `hierarchy_incomplete` lives: either (a) add it as a field on `PlanContext` and have `finalize_verdict` read `coverage['hierarchy_incomplete'] = ctx.hierarchy_incomplete` before verdict routing, or (b) specify exactly which call site in the gate run writes `coverage['hierarchy_incomplete'] = True` and when that happens relative to `finalize_verdict`'s invocation.
evidence: AC item 1: 'PlanContext/the verdict's coverage dict records whether child enumeration and child detail loading completed successfully, via a new coverage["hierarchy_incomplete"]: bool flag distinct from both has_children and llm_unavailable'
orchestrator.py:504-511: `finalize_verdict(ctx: PlanContext, ..., coverage: dict[str, Any], ...)` — coverage is a separate parameter, not a field on PlanContext
orchestrator.py:188-211: `_assemble_context_uncached` returns a `PlanContext` — it never constructs or returns a coverage dict
ABSENCE: the plan specifies no threading mechanism (return value, side-channel, or PlanContext field) for conveying `hierarchy_incomplete` from assembly to the coverage dict
findings.finding: The plan conflates `PlanContext` (the context dataclass) with `coverage` (a separate runtime dict passed into `finalize_verdict`) in AC item 1: 'PlanContext/the verdict's coverage dict records whether child enumeration ... completed ... via a new coverage["hierarchy_incomplete"]: bool flag'. These are two distinct objects in the existing code: PlanContext is assembled by `_assemble_context_uncached` and has no `coverage` dict, while `coverage` is a separate dict built during the gate run and passed to `finalize_verdict`. The plan never specifies how `hierarchy_incomplete` travels from the assembly site (where the exception is caught) into the `coverage` dict that `finalize_verdict` receives.
findings.location: AC item 1; Remediation steps 1-3; orchestrator.py lines 163-211 (_assemble_context_uncached) vs lines 503-540 (finalize_verdict signature)
scenarios: Implementer adds `hierarchy_incomplete` to `PlanContext` as a dataclass field (logical given AC item 1's 'PlanContext/...' phrasing) but `finalize_verdict` reads from `coverage`, not from `ctx` — so the flag is never seen by the verdict routing logic.
Implementer adds `hierarchy_incomplete` directly into `coverage` at assembly time but `coverage` is constructed elsewhere in the gate run, so the flag is missing when `finalize_verdict` is called.

findings.finding: The plan asserts 'verify.py's chunk retry loop' as the reference retry pattern to match when implementing bounded retries for hierarchy reads, but verify.py contains no retry loop. Its verify_findings() function iterates chunks and DEGRADES on failure (appends [] and continues with the next chunk) — it never retries a failed chunk. The cited exemplar does not exist, leaving AC#2 ('bounded retries matching this package's existing retry pattern') without a concrete reference implementation.
findings.location: Remediation §2 / AC line 2: 'bounded retries (matching this package's existing retry pattern, e.g. verify.py's chunk retry loop)'
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) identify a real retry pattern in the package to reference (search the broader rebar codebase for a bounded retry helper), or (b) specify the retry directly: 'attempt child-list/child-fetch up to N times (e.g. 3) with brief back-off; on exhaustion, set hierarchy_incomplete=True' — removing the false verify.py reference.

findings.finding: The plan directs implementers to model bounded retries on 'verify.py's chunk retry loop', but that loop retries LLM structured-output calls (StructuredOutputError on model invocations), not synchronous filesystem/git-based ticket store reads. The _reads.py facade has no retry infrastructure for store reads — it maps ReadError directly to RebarError with no retry. There is no existing analogous pattern in the package to 'match', so the claim is an unverified capability assertion that will mislead implementation.
findings.location: Remediation §2 — 'Bounded retries with actionable diagnostics'; AC2 — 'matching this package's existing retry pattern'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the analogy with a concrete specification: e.g. 'use a bare `for attempt in range(N)` loop catching `RebarError` (and optionally `OSError`) on `_reads.list_tickets` / `_reads.show_ticket`, with N=3 and no backoff (these are local file reads, not network calls); there is no existing store-read retry scaffold to match — this is new code.'

findings.finding: The plan cites 'verify.py's chunk retry loop' as the existing retry pattern to match for bounded ticket-store retries, but no such loop exists in verify.py. The verify path degrades silently on exception with no retry (lines 315-334 of verify.py show bare except → continue with no retry). The only retry infrastructure in the package is a transport-layer tenacity wrapper for LLM HTTP calls (anthropic_model.py/_build_retrying_anthropic_model), which is irrelevant to ticket-store reads. An implementer following this phantom reference has no actual pattern to copy.
findings.location: Remediation step 2: 'matching the retry pattern already used elsewhere in this package, e.g. verify.py's chunk retry loop'
findings.suggested_fix: In Remediation step 2, replace 'matching the retry pattern already used elsewhere in this package, e.g. verify.py's chunk retry loop' with a self-contained retry specification: 'a small bounded retry (e.g. up to 3 attempts with no backoff, catching the same exception classes as the enumeration/fetch try/except blocks in _assemble_context_uncached) before falling back'.

findings.finding: The plan's claimed retry-pattern analogy — 'matching the retry pattern already used elsewhere in this package, e.g. `verify.py`'s chunk retry loop' — does not describe a ticket-store I/O retry. `verify.py`'s 'chunk retry loop' is a bounded LLM structured-output reask: a single LLM call whose response fails schema validation gets one reask with the validation error fed back. There is no existing bounded-retry pattern for synchronous `_reads.show_ticket` / `_reads.list_tickets` calls in this package; the LLM transport retry (tenacity/httpx) is also unrelated. Citing a non-existent analogy as the reference implementation leaves AC2 ('bounded retries matching this package's existing retry pattern') unanchored, and an implementer may either copy the wrong retry mechanism (an LLM reask loop around a ticket read) or be unable to identify what 'matching' means.
findings.location: Remediation step 2 / AC2 — 'matching the retry pattern already used elsewhere in this package, e.g. `verify.py`'s chunk retry loop'

findings.finding: The plan conflates `PlanContext` fields and `coverage` dict keys as if they are the same storage location ('new `PlanContext`/coverage field'), but they are distinct objects: `_assemble_context_uncached` builds and caches a `PlanContext`, while `coverage` is a separate dict assembled by the workflow ops and passed into `finalize_verdict`. `finalize_verdict` reads `coverage.get('hierarchy_incomplete')` — NOT a `PlanContext` attribute. If `hierarchy_incomplete` is stored only as a `PlanContext` field, the flag must be explicitly transferred to `coverage` somewhere in the orchestration path before `finalize_verdict` is called; the plan never specifies where this transfer occurs, leaving a real implementation gap that could result in the flag being set on the context but never reaching verdict routing.
findings.location: Remediation step 1 / AC1 — 'new `PlanContext`/coverage field'
findings.suggested_fix: The cleanest fit with the existing pattern: (a) add `hierarchy_incomplete: bool = False` and `hierarchy_incomplete_detail: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)` to `PlanContext`; (b) at the start of `finalize_verdict` (or in the workflow op that calls it), write `if ctx.hierarchy_incomplete: coverage['hierarchy_incomplete'] = True; coverage['hierarchy_incomplete_detail'] = ctx.hierarchy_incomplete_detail` — making the transfer explicit and testable. Alternatively, set `coverage['hierarchy_incomplete']` directly during assembly by accepting a mutable `coverage` dict parameter, matching how other assembly steps populate coverage.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the 'matching verify.py's chunk retry loop' retry reference with a concrete, locatable anchor: either cite the actual file/function in src/rebar/llm/ that models the pattern (e.g. runner.py's bounded-retry loop for structured output, adapted for sync I/O), or specify a minimal inline pattern (e.g. 'a plain for-attempt in range(N) loop with a logged warning on each failure') so implementers have a precise, existing model to follow.
evidence: Plan text: 'recoverable child-list/child-fetch errors ... get a small bounded retry (matching the retry pattern already used elsewhere in this package, e.g. verify.py's chunk retry loop)'
ABSENCE: src/rebar/llm/plan_review/ directory listing contains no verify.py

The retry infrastructure in this codebase lives in src/rebar/llm/anthropic_model.py (_build_retrying_anthropic_model — tenacity transport) and src/rebar/llm/structured.py / runner.py (structured-output bounded retry), both scoped to LLM calls, not tracker reads
0 existing tracker-read retry callsites in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/ — Rule-of-Three not met for a new retry abstraction
findings.finding: The plan instructs implementers to apply 'bounded retries matching the retry pattern already used elsewhere in this package, e.g. verify.py's chunk retry loop' — but src/rebar/llm/plan_review/ contains no verify.py file. The package's actual retry mechanisms (tenacity transport retries in anthropic_model.py; structured-output bounded retries in runner.py/structured.py) are LLM-call patterns that do not apply to synchronous tracker reads (show_ticket / list_tickets). Without a concrete existing callsite to match, an implementer is likely to invent a new standalone retry loop — introducing a new mechanism with no prior usage in this package (Rule-of-Three: 0 existing callsites for tracker-read retry in plan_review/).
findings.location: Remediation step 2: 'Bounded retries with actionable diagnostics'
scenarios: Implementer cannot find the referenced pattern, writes a new ad-hoc retry loop using tenacity or a raw for-loop, creating an NIH mechanism with no prior usage
Implementer copies the LLM-call bounded-retry pattern (StructuredOutputError / UnretryableOutputError) and wires it to tracker-read exceptions it was never designed for

findings.finding: The plan instructs implementers to add 'bounded retries' for `_reads.list_tickets` / `_reads.show_ticket` failures and explicitly cites 'verify.py's chunk retry loop' as the matching existing pattern. Examination shows verify.py does NOT retry — it degrades silently (catch → append `[]` → continue, with no re-attempt). No existing retry pattern for synchronous local git reads exists anywhere in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/`. All actual retry infrastructure in the codebase is Tenacity-based at the HTTP transport layer (`anthropic_model.py`, `dispatch_one.py`). Citing a degrade-not-retry pattern as the prior art for building a retry loop is cargo-cult specification: the implementer would build new infrastructure unsupported by the cited precedent, for a read layer (local git orphan-branch, synchronous) whose failure mode as transient/retriable is not documented.
findings.location: Remediation step 2 / AC line 'Recoverable list/fetch failures receive bounded retries (matching this package's existing retry pattern)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC2's retry language with: 'Recoverable list/fetch failures set `hierarchy_incomplete=True` immediately (matching `_assemble_context_uncached`'s existing broad-except-with-logging pattern at orchestrator.py:175-187); the bounded-retry language and the verify.py citation are dropped since local git reads do not fail transiently and no retry pattern for this layer exists in the package.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the two function-name references in Remediation §4 and AC item 4: replace 'xcheck.apply_contradictions' → 'xcheck.apply_contradiction_drops' and 'xcheck.apply_comment_trail' → 'xcheck.apply_comment_trail_drops' (the actual symbols in xcheck.py at lines 126 and 258 respectively).
evidence: Plan text (Remediation §4): 'xcheck.apply_contradictions (line 180), xcheck.apply_comment_trail (line 311)'
Plan text (AC item 4): 'via any of its three call sites (drift_floor.apply_to_verdict, xcheck.apply_contradictions, xcheck.apply_comment_trail)'
xcheck.py search result: function at line 126 is 'apply_contradiction_drops', calls _recompute_verdict_after_drop at line 180
xcheck.py search result: function at line 258 is 'apply_comment_trail_drops', calls _recompute_verdict_after_drop at line 311
grep for 'apply_contradictions' returns no matches; grep for 'apply_comment_trail' at definition scope returns only 'apply_comment_trail_drops' and 'maybe_apply_comment_trail'
findings.finding: The plan names 'xcheck.apply_contradictions' and 'xcheck.apply_comment_trail' as the call sites of _recompute_verdict_after_drop, but the actual function names in xcheck.py are 'apply_contradiction_drops' (line 180) and 'apply_comment_trail_drops' (line 311). The AC and Remediation step 4 must add the hierarchy_incomplete branch to those real functions; using the wrong names in the plan risks the implementer searching for non-existent symbols and missing the correct functions.
findings.location: Remediation step 4 / AC item 4 — references 'xcheck.apply_contradictions' and 'xcheck.apply_comment_trail'
scenarios: An implementer who searches xcheck.py for 'apply_contradictions' or 'apply_comment_trail' to add the hierarchy_incomplete branch will find nothing and may skip those functions, leaving _recompute_verdict_after_drop without the new branch at those two call sites.
A code-review of the PR verifying against the AC may flag the implementation as non-compliant because the named functions do not exist, stalling the merge.
findings.suggested_fix: In Remediation §4: '…via any of its three call sites (drift_floor.apply_to_verdict, xcheck.apply_contradiction_drops, xcheck.apply_comment_trail_drops)…'. In AC item 4: replace the same two names identically.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the leaf's 'no relevance exception' posture with parent epic AC4: either (a) update the parent epic to drop the 'unless demonstrably irrelevant' clause first (which re-triggers the parent's plan-review attestation), then re-review this leaf; or (b) restore a scoped relevance-exception path in this leaf consistent with the parent's AC4.
evidence: Parent AC4 (6eca-183e-1cd2-4fb9): 'Plan review cannot produce an ordinary clean pass when the ticket hierarchy was loaded incompletely, unless the omitted context is demonstrably irrelevant.'
Leaf Remediation §5: 'No relevance exception. The originally proposed
deterministic
tested proof that the omitted context is irrelevant
carve-out is dropped — ... A remaining incomplete hierarchy is always INDETERMINATE
full stop.'
Leaf AC5: 'There is no relevance exception — incomplete hierarchy is unconditionally INDETERMINATE.'
The leaf's scope is broader than a consistent narrowing: it actively removes an exception that the parent's contract requires to exist.
findings.finding: The leaf unconditionally drops the 'unless demonstrably irrelevant' exception from incomplete-hierarchy handling, directly contradicting parent epic AC4 which explicitly preserves that carve-out.
findings.location: Leaf Remediation §5 ('No relevance exception') and leaf AC5 ('There is no relevance exception — incomplete hierarchy is unconditionally INDETERMINATE') vs. parent epic AC4 ('Plan review cannot produce an ordinary clean pass when the ticket hierarchy was loaded incompletely, unless the omitted context is demonstrably irrelevant.')
scenarios: A reviewer reading only the parent epic would expect a pass to be possible when omitted context is demonstrably irrelevant; the leaf silently removes that path without the parent being updated first.
Any future sibling story that implements the 'demonstrably irrelevant' case would be in direct conflict with this leaf's delivered behavior, creating an unresolvable contradiction within the epic.
If the leaf ships as written, the parent epic's AC4 will never be satisfiable as written — its attestation will require re-review against a changed implementation.
findings.suggested_fix: Update parent epic 6eca-183e-1cd2-4fb9 AC4 to read: 'Plan review cannot produce an ordinary clean pass when the ticket hierarchy was loaded incompletely.' (removing the 'unless demonstrably irrelevant' clause), thereby re-triggering the parent's plan-review attestation. Only after the parent is updated can this leaf legitimately adopt the unconditional-INDETERMINATE posture in AC5.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the retry-pattern reference in Remediation Step 2 from 'elsewhere in this package, e.g. `verify.py`' to the correct location `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py` (outside the plan_review package).
evidence: Plan says: 'matching the retry pattern already used elsewhere in this package, e.g. verify.py's chunk retry loop'
Directory listing of src/rebar/llm/plan_review/: no verify.py present.
The chunk retry loop is in src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py (a different package).
findings.finding: The plan cites `verify.py`'s chunk retry loop as the retry-pattern exemplar 'elsewhere in this package', but there is no `verify.py` in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/`. The retry chunk loop lives in `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py`, a sibling package, not the plan_review package.
findings.location: Remediation Step 2: 'matching the retry pattern already used elsewhere in this package, e.g. verify.py's chunk retry loop'
scenarios: Developer looks inside src/rebar/llm/plan_review/ for a verify.py to reference the retry pattern and finds nothing, stalling implementation or leading them to copy the wrong pattern.
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'elsewhere in this package, e.g. `verify.py`'s chunk retry loop' to 'in `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py`'s chunk retry loop (a sibling package)'.

evidence: vague/subjective terms present: clean
findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add hierarchy_incomplete to the AC's list of mirrors to update (alongside drift_floor._recompute_verdict_after_drop), explicitly calling out docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/e6_metrics.py::is_infra_indeterminate_verdict as a third convergent copy, or document in the plan why the experiment harness intentionally should NOT classify hierarchy_incomplete as an infra INDETERMINATE.
evidence: is_infra_indeterminate_verdict source: 'return bool(cov.get("llm_unavailable") or cov.get("verify_failed"))' — no mention of hierarchy_incomplete
Plan step 4 mirrors drift_floor._recompute_verdict_after_drop but does not mention is_infra_indeterminate_verdict

Plan 'Cascade flag' text: 'tightening failure behavior can affect claim-gate availability during transient tracker failures' — identifies the operator concern but not this specific experiment-harness consumer
e6_judge_reliability.py line 472: 'infra = M.is_infra_indeterminate_verdict(v, cov)' — this is the active re-run gating callsite
findings.finding: The plan's new hierarchy_incomplete → INDETERMINATE routing adds a third instance of the llm_unavailable/verify_failed coverage-flag mirror pattern, but omits a fourth existing mirror: docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/e6_metrics.py::is_infra_indeterminate_verdict (lines 228-237), which explicitly enumerates only llm_unavailable and verify_failed as 'infra INDETERMINATE' signals and drives the experiment's drop-and-rerun logic. The plan's 'cascade flag' note is vague and does not identify this consumer. After this ticket lands, a hierarchy_incomplete INDETERMINATE verdict will NOT be classified as infra by is_infra_indeterminate_verdict, so experiment reruns would retain it as a genuine judge-INDETERMINATE disagreement data point, silently polluting agreement metrics.
findings.location: Remediation step 3 / 'Cascade flag' note; docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/e6_metrics.py:228-237
scenarios: An experiment rerun after this ticket lands retains hierarchy_incomplete INDETERMINATE verdicts as 'genuine judge disagreements', inflating the INDETERMINATE agreement bucket and skewing judge reliability metrics

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for the test AC (e.g. `pytest tests/llm/plan_review/test_hierarchy_completeness.py -v`) and at least one grep/jq command confirming the new `elif coverage.get("hierarchy_incomplete")` branch exists in both `orchestrator.py` and `drift_floor.py`.
evidence: The AC says 'Tests cover: total child-list failure, one-child detail failure among several, retry-then-recovery...' but specifies no test file path, no pytest invocation, and no grep/jq command to confirm the branches exist in the implementation.
All seven ACs are prose-stated outcomes with no 'run X and observe Y' proving command attached.
ABSENCE: no section named 'Verification', 'How to test', or equivalent appears in the plan.
findings.finding: No concrete proving commands (pytest invocations, test file paths, grep/jq checks) are specified for any acceptance criterion. The test AC lists scenarios in prose but provides no runnable command, test file location, or CI check reference an executor can invoke to produce evidence of success.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — final bullet ('Tests cover: total child-list failure...')
scenarios: An executing agent implements all changes correctly but has no deterministic command to run to confirm completion.
A reviewer cannot distinguish 'tests written and passing' from 'tests described but not yet written' without a concrete run command.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Verification' section: (1) `pytest tests/llm/plan_review/test_hierarchy_completeness.py -v` — all listed scenarios pass; (2) `grep -n 'hierarchy_incomplete' src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py src/rebar/llm/plan_review/drift_floor.py` — both files show the new branch; (3) `python -c "from rebar.llm.plan_review.orchestrator import PlanContext; ..."` smoke-test for the new field.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the concrete retry bound in the plan (e.g. 'up to 3 attempts with 1 s linear backoff, matching verify.py's `MAX_CHUNK_RETRIES=3`') so an executor does not need to read a separate file to determine the correct value.
evidence: 'recoverable child-list/child-fetch errors ... get a small bounded retry (matching the retry pattern already used elsewhere in this package, e.g. verify.py's chunk retry loop) before falling back'
No retry count, delay, or jitter value is stated in the plan itself; 'small bounded retry' is imprecise.
findings.finding: The retry count and backoff for the new bounded retry on child-list/child-fetch errors is described only as 'matching this package's existing retry pattern (e.g. verify.py's chunk retry loop)' — the actual bound (N attempts, delay) is left for the executor to discover by reading a separate file, which is a defaultable gap that could produce inconsistent implementations.
findings.location: Remediation §2 — 'Bounded retries with actionable diagnostics'
scenarios: One implementer reads verify.py and uses 3 retries with exponential backoff; another uses 2 retries with no delay — both claim to satisfy the AC.
findings.suggested_fix: In Remediation §2, add: 'Use up to N=3 attempts (matching `verify.py`'s `MAX_CHUNK_RETRIES` constant) with a 1-second linear delay between attempts — no jitter required for this path.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit test assertions that `coverage['hierarchy_incomplete_detail']` contains the correct child IDs for a partial failure and the `'enumeration'` sentinel for a total child-list failure (not just that the verdict is INDETERMINATE).
evidence: AC bullet 6: 'The INDETERMINATE verdict's coverage detail identifies which specific hierarchy reads failed or were omitted (coverage["hierarchy_incomplete_detail"]: child ids, or "enumeration" for a total child-list failure)' — specifies content, but

ABSENCE: the test AC bullet lists 'total child-list failure, one-child detail failure among several, retry-then-recovery, finalize_verdict returns INDETERMINATE, _recompute_verdict_after_drop likewise re-derives INDETERMINATE' — none of these explicitly require asserting the *contents* of hierarchy_incomplete_detail
The detail field is described as operator-facing diagnostic enabling triage between 'transient, retry' and 'genuinely broken hierarchy read' (Cascade flag section)
findings.finding: The test AC requires verifying that `INDETERMINATE` is returned when `hierarchy_incomplete` is set, but never requires a test that the `coverage['hierarchy_incomplete_detail']` field is correctly populated — no test checks that it contains the right child IDs (for partial failures) or the 'enumeration' sentinel (for total child-list failure). This is a new operator-facing diagnostic field whose content is part of the specified observable contract, but its correctness is untested.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — last bullet (Tests cover …) / Remediation §2 and §6
scenarios: A bug populates hierarchy_incomplete_detail with an empty list or wrong child IDs; the test suite goes green because it only asserts on the verdict string, not the detail field — operators get a useless INDETERMINATE with no actionable detail
Total child-list failure sets detail to a child ID instead of 'enumeration'; untested, silently ships wrong
findings.suggested_fix: In the test AC's final bullet, add two sub-cases: (i) total child-list failure → assert `coverage['hierarchy_incomplete_detail'] == ['enumeration']`; (ii) one-child detail failure for child 'CHILD-42' among several → assert `'CHILD-42' in coverage['hierarchy_incomplete_detail']` and no other child IDs present.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a note to AGENTS.md (or an ADR/design doc) capturing the invariant: any new `coverage` flag that affects the verdict in `finalize_verdict` MUST also be mirrored in `_recompute_verdict_after_drop`, and explain why (three call sites in drift_floor/xcheck can re-derive a verdict after finalize runs).
evidence: Plan states '_recompute_verdict_after_drop gains the identical hierarchy_incomplete branch' but mentions no documentation update
Absence: no mention of ADR, AGENTS.md, or design-doc update capturing the 'mirrored verdict-recompute' contract

The plan itself notes the existing branches 'duplicates the llm_unavailable/verify_failed if/elif chain verbatim — not a separate concern' — confirming this is an established but undocumented pattern being extended
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new architectural invariant — every coverage flag that affects the verdict must appear in BOTH `finalize_verdict` AND `_recompute_verdict_after_drop` — but does not require updating any ADR, AGENTS.md, or design doc to capture this contract. Future contributors adding a new coverage flag have no documented guidance that mirroring in `_recompute_verdict_after_drop` is required, making the invariant invisible and likely to be violated.
findings.location: Remediation step 4 / AC for `_recompute_verdict_after_drop`
scenarios: A developer adds a fourth coverage flag (e.g. `tool_unavailable`) to `finalize_verdict` but misses `_recompute_verdict_after_drop`, silently allowing dropped-BLOCK-finding paths to bypass the new guard — the same bug this ticket fixes, repeated.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a section to AGENTS.md under plan-review orchestration: 'Verdict-recompute invariant: finalize_verdict and _recompute_verdict_after_drop must remain in sync — any new coverage flag added to one must be added to the other in the same relative position. See drift_floor._recompute_verdict_after_drop docstring for rationale.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a testing step (or sub-bullets within the relevant Remediation steps) that describes where and how the seven test scenarios in AC7 will be implemented, so every AC maps to described work.
evidence: Remediation steps 1–5 describe: a new flag, bounded retries, finalize_verdict branch, drift_floor mirror, and no-exception policy — none of these steps mention a test module, test file, or testing strategy.

AC7 names seven distinct test scenarios (total child-list failure, one-child detail failure, retry-then-recovery, finalize_verdict INDETERMINATE, _recompute_verdict_after_drop INDETERMINATE) with no corresponding described work in the plan body.
findings.finding: AC7 (the test coverage requirement) lists specific test scenarios as an acceptance criterion, but the Remediation body contains no testing section or description of where/how tests will be written. This creates an orphan criterion: AC7 measures work that is never described as a deliverable in the plan.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, final bullet ('Tests cover: ...')
scenarios: An implementer reads the Remediation steps, builds all five deliverables, but has no plan guidance on how or where to write the tests — the AC acts as a surprise constraint with no design backing.
A reviewer checking plan completeness cannot trace AC7 back to any described work, making the plan unverifiable without external context.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Remediation step 6 (or expand step 2/3/4 with test sub-bullets) that identifies the test module(s), notes the retry-then-recovery and verdict-routing scenarios to cover, and references the existing test infrastructure in this package — giving AC7 a concrete described deliverable to map against.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback or expand-contract mechanism for the verdict behavioral change (e.g., a feature flag to revert INDETERMINATE→PASS for hierarchy_incomplete during outages, or a documented back-out procedure), so the plan satisfies T4's reversibility requirement beyond mere acknowledgement.
evidence: Cascade flag: 'tightening failure behavior can affect claim-gate availability during transient tracker failures' — this is acknowledgement, not a rollback/reversibility mechanism.
Remediation step 3 adds a new elif branch that routes to INDETERMINATE; no mention of a feature flag, phased rollout, or back-out procedure if this causes unexpected claim-gate outages.
T4 rubric: 'The remedy for a destructive/breaking change is an explicit ROLLBACK / back-out plan or expand-contract sequencing — checking only that breakage is acknowledged is insufficient.'
findings.finding: The plan changes `finalize_verdict`'s output behavior (cases that previously returned PASS now return INDETERMINATE when hierarchy_incomplete is set), a behavioral breaking change for downstream consumers of the verdict. The Cascade flag section acknowledges the impact on claim-gate availability but provides no rollback path, back-out plan, or expand-contract sequencing — acknowledgement alone is insufficient under T4.
findings.location: Cascade flag section; Remediation step 3 (finalize_verdict branch)
scenarios: A transient but prolonged tracker outage causes a flood of INDETERMINATE verdicts, blocking all plan-review claim gates with no operator lever to revert to the previous behavior while the tracker recovers.
Post-deploy, if the INDETERMINATE routing causes unexpected downstream failures, there is no described back-out path — the team must hot-patch the elif branch with no plan-level guidance.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Require that when `hierarchy_incomplete` is set (retries exhausted), a structured log entry at WARNING or ERROR level is emitted (including child ids from `hierarchy_incomplete_detail`), so operators can aggregate and alert on hierarchy read failure rates without inspecting individual review records.
evidence: Plan specifies coverage['hierarchy_incomplete'] and coverage['hierarchy_incomplete_detail'] as the diagnostic artifacts
Absence: no mention of log.warning/log.error, metric increment, or trace event when hierarchy_incomplete is set
The cascade-flag note acknowledges operator impact ('an operator can distinguish transient, retry from genuinely broken') but the only mechanism provided is the coverage dict
findings.finding: The plan does not require emitting a structured log line or metric counter when hierarchy reads fail (after retries are exhausted and `hierarchy_incomplete` is set). The only observability artifact is the `coverage` dict inside a single review's output, which requires inspecting individual review records rather than enabling aggregate monitoring across reviews.
findings.location: Remediation step 2 / AC: 'Recoverable list/fetch failures receive bounded retries...'
scenarios: Tracker read failures begin occurring at a rate of 10% across all reviews; without a log line or metric, operators cannot detect the degradation without querying and aggregating individual review coverage dicts.
An on-call engineer is alerted to a downstream symptom (spike in INDETERMINATE verdicts) but has no log/metric to immediately identify hierarchy read failures as the cause.
findings.suggested_fix: In the retry-exhaustion code path that sets `coverage['hierarchy_incomplete'] = True`, add: `logger.warning('plan_review.hierarchy_incomplete', extra={'child_ids': failed_ids, 'plan_id': plan_id})` — matching whatever structured logging convention the package already uses.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the `hierarchy_incomplete_detail` value vocabulary as named constants (e.g. `HIERARCHY_INCOMPLETE_ENUMERATION = "enumeration"`) in the plan, and specify that both production code and tests reference these constants rather than bare string literals.
evidence: Plan text: 'record which specific reads failed or were omitted (e.g. coverage["hierarchy_incomplete_detail"]: list[str] — child ids or "enumeration" for a total failure)'

AC item 6: 'the INDETERMINATE verdict's coverage detail identifies which specific hierarchy reads failed or were omitted (coverage["hierarchy_incomplete_detail"]: child ids, or "enumeration" for a total child-list failure)'
ABSENCE: no named constant, enum, or vocabulary table is specified for these sentinel values anywhere in the plan
findings.finding: The `coverage['hierarchy_incomplete_detail']` value vocabulary is partially specified — the plan says it contains 'child ids or "enumeration" for a total child-list failure' — but the sentinel string `"enumeration"` is never defined as a named constant or enum member. Any code consuming or testing this field must hard-code the string, and tests must know the exact sentinel value to assert against.
findings.location: Remediation step 2: 'coverage["hierarchy_incomplete_detail"]: list[str] — child ids or "enumeration" for a total failure'; AC item 6
scenarios: Test code asserts `assert 'enumeration' in coverage['hierarchy_incomplete_detail']` while production code writes `'child_list_failure'` — a silent mismatch that passes tests against a mock but fails in production.

A downstream operator tool parsing `hierarchy_incomplete_detail` for alerting cannot reliably distinguish total-enumeration failure from per-child failure without the exact sentinel string being contractually fixed.

findings.finding: `drift_floor._recompute_verdict_after_drop` is an unenumerated co-edit target: it mirrors `finalize_verdict`'s verdict-recomputation rule verbatim (confirmed by its own docstring) and checks `llm_unavailable`/`verify_failed` but will NOT check `hierarchy_incomplete`. After any drift/xcheck floor drops a BLOCK finding post-`finalize_verdict`, this function re-derives the verdict from scratch and can flip it to `PASS` even when `hierarchy_incomplete` is set — bypassing the new INDETERMINATE guard. It is called from three sites: `drift_floor.apply_to_verdict` (line 272), `xcheck.apply_contradictions` (line 180), and `xcheck.apply_comment_trail` (line 311).
findings.location: src/rebar/llm/plan_review/drift_floor.py:190-211 (`_recompute_verdict_after_drop`); also xcheck.py:180, xcheck.py:311
findings.suggested_fix: In `drift_floor._recompute_verdict_after_drop`, after the `elif cov.get('verify_failed'):` block and before the final `else: verdict['verdict'] = 'PASS'`, add: `elif cov.get('hierarchy_incomplete'): verdict['verdict'] = 'INDETERMINATE'`. Mirror this in the plan's Remediation and AC so all three call sites (drift_floor.py:272, xcheck.py:180, xcheck.py:311) inherit the fix through the shared function.

findings.finding: `drift_floor._recompute_verdict_after_drop` (orchestrator.py:503 parallel copy at drift_floor.py:190-211) replicates `finalize_verdict`'s coverage-flag if/elif chain verbatim but is not mentioned anywhere in the plan's remediation. Adding `elif coverage.get('hierarchy_incomplete'): verdict = 'INDETERMINATE'` to `finalize_verdict` without adding the matching branch to `_recompute_verdict_after_drop` means a verdict that was downgraded from BLOCK by the drift floor will re-derive to PASS (the else branch at line 211) even when `hierarchy_incomplete` is set — a silent bypass of the new guard in exactly the convergent re-review path.
findings.location: Remediation step 3 / AC item 3: specifies only `finalize_verdict`; src/rebar/llm/plan_review/drift_floor.py lines 190-211
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Remediation step 3: 'Also add the matching `elif coverage.get("hierarchy_incomplete"): verdict = "INDETERMINATE"` branch to `drift_floor._recompute_verdict_after_drop` (drift_floor.py:190), which mirrors `finalize_verdict`'s branch logic and must stay in sync with it.' Add a corresponding AC item: 'drift_floor._recompute_verdict_after_drop also routes to INDETERMINATE when hierarchy_incomplete is set.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
